meta.title: Iceland Ring Road — South & Golden Circle
meta.subtitle: 5-day campervan loop · Jul 1–5, 2026
meta.timezoneNote: Iceland = NYC + 4 hrs (Iceland stays on GMT year-round). Early-July daylight ~3 AM–midnight, never fully dark.
meta.clockNote: All times are 24-hour, Iceland local (GMT). Each stop stores an explicit `arrive` plus an editable `duration` (minutes). `arrive` is computed from the day's `dayStart` + preceding durations + drive legs; the app recomputes it live from real Google Directions drive times and can save changes back here. `driveToNext` is null in this file — the app fills it from the live Directions leg.

flights.outbound: Delta DL246 — JFK 11:50 PM Tue Jun 30 → KEF 9:30 AM Wed Jul 1
flights.return: Delta DL247 — KEF 11:25 AM Sun Jul 5 → JFK 1:27 PM

budget.note: Rough per-person on-the-ground cost (excludes flights + camper rental). Iceland removed fuel taxes Jan 2026, so diesel dropped to ~260 ISK/L. Skip the Camping Card — not worth it under ~10 nights.

perPerson.bluLagoon: $100–130 (the big one — BOOK NOW, sells out)
perPerson.parkingFees: ~$30 (7 paid lots × ~$7, split across the car)

mustBookAhead: Jökulsárlón boat tour if you want it (a few days)

logistics: Day 1: after a 9:30 AM landing you won't be on the road until ~11:30 AM (peak-summer customs + camper-depot shuttle). No firm plans before midday.
Blue Lagoon is on Day 1 (arrival) — 20 min from KEF, the recommended & only feasible slot given the early departure. Book ~12:00 PM entry as a buffer.

LATE-EVENING RHYTHM (Days 1–3): early-July daylight runs ~03:10 sunrise to ~23:50 sunset and never gets dark, so Days 1–3 deliberately run into the long evening — the last real activity ends ~22:00 in the low midnight-sun light, then a short drive/wind-down, bedtime ~23:30, and a sleep-in to ~08:30 wake (no alarms). Day 1 still starts 09:30 (fixed 09:30 landing); Days 2–3 start ~09:30/10:30 off an ~08:30 wake plus morning routine + drive to the first stop.

SLEEP RULE: 9 hours of sleep every night, preserved on the late shift. Each day's `overnight` has an explicit bedtime/wake/sleepHours block, and the next day's dayStart leaves room for 9h sleep + morning routine + drive to the first stop. Days 1–3: bed ~23:30 → wake ~08:30 (9h). The app warns (amber/red) if editing durations pushes bedtime so late that 9h before the required wake becomes impossible.

TRADEOFF — last night (Jul 4→5) stays EARLY: the 07:30 camper drop (at the Fitjabraut depot, ~5 min from KEF) forces a ~06:00 wake, so to still get 9h you go to bed ~21:00. Day 4 (Golden Circle) is therefore the exception to the late rhythm — it starts early (09:00, off a 07:30 wake on Night 3) and winds down ~19:00 at Reykjadalur (with dinner in Hveragerði) so you're at the Keflavík base by ~20:30 and in bed by 21:00. Night 3's overnight (bed 22:30 / wake 07:30) is the bridge: it's earlier than Days 1–2 precisely so Day 4 can start early. This is the one night 9h forces an early bedtime.

Day 5: wake ~06:00, fill the tank at the N1 by the depot, drop the camper at the 7:30 AM slot (Fitjabraut depot, NOT the terminal — ~5 min away; drop-off takes 30–45 min). Aim to be at KEF by ~08:35, ~2h50m before the 11:25 flight — July is a KEF peak month and US flights are time-sensitive, so this buffer matters. If the depot shuttle is off-cycle, take a taxi. ⚠️ Verify with the rental: depot opens by 7:30, and the drop DATE is Jul 5 (your booking showed Jul 6 — confirm).

days.title: Arrival, Blue Lagoon & Waterfalls

overnight.note: Night 1 base — large, clean campground in Vík. Late-evening rhythm: in after Skógafoss ~22:30, wind down, bed 23:30 → wake 08:30 (9h). Reynisfjara is 10 min away, so a 09:30 Day-2 start is relaxed.

days.narr: Land 9:30 AM, clear customs, pick up the camper (~11:30 AM). Soak off the red-eye at the Blue Lagoon, then head east on Route 1 for two of the South Coast's biggest waterfalls before the black-sand coast at Vík. Easy first day — you'll be jet-lagged, and the long evening light means no rush.

stops.note: Land. Clear customs (allow 30–45 min, peak morning). Pick up campervan via depot shuttle — buy duty-free alcohol + grab a SIM here first. ~2 hrs from wheels-down to on the road.

🍳 POST-LANDING BREAKFAST — a real bakery, not airport food. Big pastry/bread selection, free coffee refills, local favorite in Njarðvík (Hólagata 17). Open daily 7:00–18:00, so it's ready when you roll off the red-eye. Right on the way from KEF toward the Blue Lagoon. (Alt: Sigurjónsbakarí in Keflavík.)

stops.bookNote: MANDATORY timed entry — walk-ins never accepted. Sells out 3–4 weeks ahead for July. Comfort ticket from ~11,990 ISK but July midday slots run higher (dynamic pricing); cheapest slots are 8am or after 8pm Tue–Thu. Book NOW.
stops.note: Moved to arrival day — it's 20 min from KEF and the perfect post-red-eye soak. Quick bakery breakfast first, then book ~12:15 entry for buffer against customs delays. On-site luggage storage (~$6/bag) or leave bags in the camper. A long ~4 hr soak to reset after the red-eye. 🦞 LUNCH option ~10 min away in Grindavík: Bryggjan (famous lobster soup, reopened June 2025) — better than the on-site cafés; or Max's @ Northern Light Inn ~4 min away as the reliable fallback. Eat after your soak before heading east.
The one you can walk BEHIND. You'll get soaked — full rain gear. Paid parking. Evening light is great and crowds thin out — no rush in the midnight sun.
Massive and thunderous, rainbows on sunny days. Climb the stairs to the top viewpoint — you've got light until ~midnight, so linger into the low evening light (~10 PM finish).

BIG SHOP for the whole east leg — you'll cook at the Vík and Svínafell campsite kitchens. Krónan is the supermarket in Vík (there's NO Bónus here), on the main road by the N1. Open ~10–20. Stock up before the east coast gets sparse. Top off fuel at the N1/Orkan next door too. 🍔 DINNER (the trip's tightest food moment): Smiðjan Brugghús (brewpub, burgers/ribs) or Suður-Vík (sit-down lamb/char) — BUT both kitchens close ~21:00 and you arrive Vík ~22:00. Either arrive by ~20:45 (trim a waterfall), call ahead, or just cook at the campsite tonight with what you grabbed here.

stops.bookNote: First-come, large site (250 capacity) — rarely fills, just arrive.
stops.note: Night 1 base. Large, clean, cozy common kitchen. Showers ~300kr (coins at reception). Crowded in summer. In ~23:05, wind down, bed ~23:30. Reynisfjara is 10 min away — hit it first thing tomorrow.

days.title: Black Sand, Canyons, Glaciers & Icebergs

overnight.note: Night 2 base — overnight east at Svínafell to avoid backtracking. Adding Dyrhólaey (puffins) pushed Day 2 ~40 min later, so this is the latest night: in ~23:20, bed ~23:50 → wake 08:50 to keep the full 9h (Day 3 is a relaxed light-driving day, so a slightly later wake is fine — it just nudges the 10:30 first stop). If you'd rather not run this late, trim the puffin stop or shave time at Svartifoss/Diamond Beach.

days.narr: Start at the black beach right by Vík, then the most dramatic stretch of the trip: a slot canyon, the Skaftafell glacier area, and the famous iceberg lagoon + Diamond Beach. Overnight east at Svínafell to avoid backtracking — the move that makes this day work.

stops.note: Moved here from Day 1 — it's 10 min from Vík where you slept, so it's the natural first stop. Basalt columns + sea stacks. DANGER: sneaker waves kill people here. Never turn your back on the ocean.

🐦 PUFFIN spot — peak nesting season is exactly now (June–early Aug). Dramatic promontory with a lighthouse + rock arch, ~10 min from Reynisfjara so basically no detour. Lower viewpoint always open + has puffins; the UPPER lighthouse area sometimes closes during nesting to protect the birds (and the access road can need higher clearance — check the gate). Bring a zoom lens. Free, ~30–40 min.
Serpentine slot canyon (the 'Justin Bieber canyon'). ~1 hr+ round trip on the rim trail. Pay parking or you'll be fined.

⛽ THE most important fill of the trip. This is the LAST reliable fuel before the long empty stretch past Skaftafell to Jökulsárlón and back — there's nothing until Höfn, well east of the lagoon. Top up even if you're half-full. 2 min off the route, right by the canyon. 🍽️ LUNCH right here: Systrakaffi (Klausturvegur 13, open daily 12:00–21:00, Arctic char/pizza/cakes, no reservations) — ideal ~1pm drop-in. Or hold out for the Glacier Goodies food truck at Skaftafell (langoustine soup / fish & chips, ~11:30–20:00).
Part of Vatnajökull National Park. Quick glacier-view stop + trailhead for Svartifoss. (Do NOT book a guided glacier walk today — that's 6–8 hrs and would kill the lagoon.)
The 'black waterfall' over hexagonal basalt columns — same Skaftafell trailhead. ~2 hrs round trip at an easy pace. Pair with the glacier viewpoint.

stops.bookNote: Lagoon viewing + parking is cheap. OPTIONAL amphibian boat tour ~7,100 ISK/$51 pp (every ~30 min, popular slots fill — book a few days ahead); Zodiac ~$120 pp books out fast. Boat not required to enjoy it.
stops.note: Icebergs calving off the glacier drift across the lagoon. Watch for seals. Boat tours available. A trip highlight — give it real time in the long evening light. 🦞 DINNER call: skip the 1h40m round-trip detour east to Höfn for langoustine (it's mostly imported now anyway). Instead eat at the Heimahumar 'Local Langoustine' food truck RIGHT HERE at the lagoon (langoustine roll + acclaimed soup, genuinely local) — but it closes before 7pm, so make it an early ~5:30–6pm dinner before Diamond Beach. Sit-down backup nearby: Frost @ Fjallsárlón (~15 min west) or cook at Svínafell tonight.
Ice chunks wash up like diamonds on black sand. Go at LOW TIDE for the classic look. Across the road from the lagoon. Evening light is gorgeous here — finishes ~22:30 in the golden low-sun glow.
Night 2 base — repeatedly called the best site on the South Coast. ~$12pp. Great kitchen, clean showers. ~50 min back west of the lagoon; in ~23:20, bed shortly after.

days.title: Transit West + Hot Spring

overnight.note: Night 3 base — camp in Flúðir right at the Secret Lagoon. This night PRECEDES the early Day 4, so it stays earlier: a long evening soak ends ~21:35, bed 22:30 → wake 07:30 (9h); Brúarfoss is ~1h away for a 09:00 Day-4 start.

days.narr: Your longest drive, heading back west to set up the Golden Circle. Light on stops by design, so sleep in — you've earned it. Fuel up; long gaps between stations. A wild-pool soak at Seljavallalaug on the way, then a long, unhurried evening soak at the Secret Lagoon.

stops.note: Historic pool hidden in a valley, 20-min walk in. Lukewarm, not hot, rough changing rooms. A quiet, unhurried wild soak to start a light driving day. ⛽ Fuel before this leg — top off at the N1 or Orkan in Vík when you leave (Orkan is cheaper + 24/7); it's a long drive west with thin stations.

Resupply for the Golden Circle back half — Bónus is Iceland's cheapest chain (open 10–20). Cook at the Flúðir/Reykjavík-area campsites. Selfoss is also the big fuel + services hub (N1 Selfoss) on the way west, so refuel here too if you didn't in Vík. 🍽️ LUNCH in Selfoss: Kaffi Krús (burgers/pizza/cakes, good value, ~11–22) or Tryggvaskáli (Iceland's oldest restaurant building, more refined; from 11:30). Grab-and-go: GK Bakarí.
Volcanic crater lake with a red rim — small entry fee. Walk the rim + down to the water at an easy pace. Toilets close 9pm.

stops.bookNote: Lagoon: 4,500 ISK/$33 pp, book a day or two ahead in summer (towel/suit rental ~$8). Flúðir campsite right here ~$16 pp + tax, no booking needed.
stops.note: Iceland's oldest pool — less touristy than Blue/Sky, ~$35pp. Reservation recommended. With the short driving day, settle in for a long ~4 hr evening soak in the midnight-sun light, ending ~21:15. Camp right here in Flúðir for Night 3 — bed ~22:30 (earlier, because Day 4 starts early). EAT-OUT option: Minilik, an affordable Ethiopian spot right here in Flúðir (big portions, great for veg; Tue–Sun 18:00–21:00) — perfect since you're parked here.

days.title: The Golden Circle

overnight.place: Happy Campers Keflavík (for the early Day-5 drop)
overnight.note: Night 4 base — Happy Campers Keflavík campsite (~$14 pp), closest to the depot for the morning drop. TIGHT NIGHT: the 07:30 depot drop means wake ~06:00 (pack up + ~10 min drive to Fitjabraut by 07:30), so bed by 21:00 for 9h. That's why Day 4 winds down earlier (be at this base by ~20:30).

days.narr: The classic trio plus a blue-water bonus. Þingvellir and Geysir are the heart of the Golden Circle. With the long evening you can do the full Reykjadalur hot-river hike and still finish in daylight, then drive toward Keflavík so the morning camper drop is painless.

stops.note: Unreal turquoise-blue water. Use the closer parking lot for a 30–45 min round trip (the old trailhead is ~1 hr each way — skip that long version).
The 'Golden Falls' — a thundering two-tier monster. Upper + lower platforms. Café on site for lunch.

Erupts every 5–10 min, up to 30m. Free (pay parking). Wait for a couple of eruptions; walk uphill for the geothermal field view. 🍅 LUNCH — Friðheimar tomato-greenhouse restaurant (Reykholt, ~10 min away): eat among the vines, unlimited tomato-soup-+-bread buffet ~3,740 ISK ($27), kitchen 11:30–16:00. BOOKING REQUIRED — sells out weeks ahead in July, reserve NOW. No-booking fallback: their on-site Winebar/Bistro, or Gullfoss Kaffi (refillable lamb soup) / Efstidalur II farm (burgers + cowshed-view ice cream, best value).
Walk between the North American & Eurasian tectonic plates; site of the original Icelandic parliament + Öxarárfoss. The biggest Golden Circle time block (~2 hrs). Pay parking ~1000kr.

stops.bookNote: River is free; pay ~700 ISK parking via the Parka app. Night-4 base: Happy Campers Keflavík ~$14 pp + tax, no booking — closest convenient site for the early airport drop.
stops.note: A naturally hot river up a ~3km uphill hike each way. Trimmed to ~3 hrs (vs the full 3.5–5) so you finish ~19:00. 🍕 DINNER right here in Hveragerði before the push west: Ölverk Pizza & Brewery (wood-fired pizza + geothermal in-house brews; kitchen closes ~21:00 so don't arrive after 8:45). Then drive ~50 min to the Keflavík base — bed ~21:00 for the early 06:00 wake before the 07:30 camper drop. If you'd rather linger in the river, you'll cut into sleep. Closer-to-KEF dinner alt: Kaffi Duus in Keflavík (harbourside seafood, kitchen to 22:00). Trailhead at Hveragerði, on the way west toward Keflavík.

days.title: Departure
days.narr: Clean exit. No activities. Wake ~6:00, pack up, fill the tank at the N1 by the depot, drop the camper at the 7:30 AM slot (Fitjabraut depot, ~5 min from KEF), get to the terminal by ~8:35 — ~2h50m before the 11:25 flight. Breakfast: Hotel Keflavík's KEF Café opens 5:00 AM (the only real hot food that early); otherwise eat at the airport after security.

stops.note: Drop at the Fitjabraut 3 depot (NOT the terminal — it's ~3 km / 5 min away). Drop-off takes 30–45 min: walk-around inspection, fuel check, paperwork. ⛽ FILL THE TANK FIRST at the N1 next door (Fitjabraut 2) — the refuel surcharge is brutal. 📸 PHOTOGRAPH every panel/windscreen/tire before handing over keys (gravel chips are the #1 dispute). The free airport shuttle is HOURLY + pre-booked — if your inspection finishes off-cycle, just grab a taxi (~5 min, ~3,000–4,000 ISK) rather than wait. ⚠️ VERIFY with the rental (+354 539 6479): (a) depot opens by 7:30, (b) drop-off DATE is Jul 5 not Jul 6 — your booking form showed Jul 6, confirm it matches your flight day.

At the terminal ~2h50m before the 11:25 AM Delta flight (DL247). July is a KEF peak month + US-bound flights are the most time-sensitive — Isavia wants you here 2.5–3 hrs early. Security 6–9 AM is the rush (30–50 min). Hit the VAT-refund/tax-free desk early if claiming it (slow in summer). Fly home.
